title: Payment Processing SLA Policy — Targets and Severity Levels
content:
Payment Processing SLA Policy

Availability Target: 99.95% monthly uptime for payment authorization and settlement.

Latency Targets:
- P50 authorization latency: < 200ms
- P95 authorization latency: < 500ms
- P99 authorization latency: < 1000ms
- End-to-end settlement confirmation: < 30 seconds

Error Rate Target: < 0.1% of transactions per rolling 5-minute window.

Severity Levels:

Sev-1 (Critical):
- Complete payment outage or > 50% transaction failure rate.
- Response: Immediate page, Incident Commander within 10 min, executive notify within 30 min.
- Resolution target: 1 hour.

Sev-2 (Major):
- Degraded performance: P95 latency 500ms–2000ms or error rate 0.1%–1%.
- Response: On-call ack within 15 min, status updates every 30 min.
- Resolution target: 4 hours.

Sev-3 (Minor):
- Isolated errors affecting < 5% of traffic or single merchant/rail.
- Response: Ticket queue, business-hours remediation.
- Resolution target: 24 hours.

Sev-4 (Informational):
- Non-customer-impacting anomalies, monitoring gaps.
- Response: Backlog item, no page.

Measurement: SLIs computed from production metrics in Grafana Payments-Prod dashboard. Breaches trigger automated alerts per Payment Outage Runbook.
